What did Miss Rossi plan to do once the recycling box was full?
options: give the things to the children
use the things in the classroom
take the box to the recycling centre
share the things with the other teachers

questions.text: What did Rosila bring to school?
Mani brought some things to put in the recycling box. What were the things made from?
Everyone brought something. What does this sentence do?
Why was the recycling box empty the next morning?
options: The teachers had taken the things to use themselves.
Miss Rossi had given the things to the other teachers.
The cleaners had emptied the box into the recycling bin.
The children had taken the things to the recycling centre.

questions.text: (OH-SE-LI) This word is written like this to show
options: the correct way to say ocelli.
a different way to spell ocelli.
how many letters are in ocelli.

questions.text: Why was the boy’s father worried?
options: His son was not strong enough.
His son was always getting lost.
His son had never had any problems.

questions.text: Why did the father send his son into the forest?
options: He wanted to punish his son.
He was fighting with his son.
He was too old to go himself.
He wanted his son to learn.

questions.text: What did the boy do first when the cart broke?
Which word best describes the boy’s father in this story?
What is the main message of this story?
options: Family is the most important thing.
Working hard makes you a better person.
It is important to know how to solve problems.

questions.text: Did the father know a person called Necessity? Give a reason for your answer.
I know lots of kids say they’re not interested in competitions ... What reason does Liz give for this?
options: They don’t like any activity that involves exercise.
They don’t have enough confidence in themselves.
They haven’t competed in any competitions before.
They haven’t experienced what it’s like to come first.

questions.text: Which of these points do Liz and Sam both make?
options: Kids should try to win more.

questions.text: Liz suggests that kids who don’t like competitions should
options: let others compete in their place.
be allowed to play a different sport.

questions.text: What is one reason Liz gives for saying a garden is a really bad idea?
options: Some students will be left out.
Some students don’t like getting dirty.
Some students don’t enjoy being outside.
Some students already know how to garden.

questions.text: People always say kids don’t get enough exercise. Why does Liz say this?
options: to show she’s considering other arguments

questions.text: And think about it: what helps you to be good at sport? Sam asks this question to
options: find an answer to the question.

questions.text: Have you ever had an idea that just pops into your head out of nowhere? Who is Tim asking?
What is Tim’s secret plan?
options: to make a model of a thylacine
to train Elvis to find a thylacine
to make Elvis look like a thylacine

questions.text: But I feel funny about saying I own him ... What reason does Tim give for feeling this way?
options: Tim thinks dogs cannot be owned.
All the family helps to look after Elvis.
Elvis is much older than Tim in dog years.

questions.text: Why does Tim want his plan to be secret?
options: He is not really going to do it.
He doesn’t want Greer to copy him.
He thinks people will make fun of him.

questions.text: What does this text show us about Tim?
options: He is good at drawing animals.
He is good at running long distances.

questions.text: The text comes from a book that is meant to be funny. Give an example from the text that suggests humour. Explain how this could be funny.
How does this story end?
This story is not true. Use information from the text to give a reason for this opinion.
